_comment: CloudWatch alarms for Clew v2 Thermia metrics. Deploy with aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm or Terraform aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm resources. All 9 alerts from the Thermia consultation Part 3.

alarms.AlarmDescription: ConversationManager miss rate >40% sustained 10 minutes. Runbook: Check if post-deploy (suppress 15m). If sustained, check Slack API health and thread TTL. PDR-1: extend TTL to 4h if miss rate >20% sustained.
alarms.Notes: Computed as miss_total / (miss_total + hit_total) over 10m window. Requires CloudWatch Math Expression: METRICS('m1') / (METRICS('m1') + METRICS('m2')) where m1=miss_total, m2=hit_total. Suppress for 15m after clew_startup_timestamp changes.

alarms.AlarmDescription: ConversationManager miss rate >80% sustained 5 minutes. CRITICAL: near-total cache failure. Immediate investigation required. Check if Slack API is down or if a deploy loop is occurring.
alarms.Notes: Same math expression as miss-rate-high but tighter window (5m) and higher threshold (80%). Suppress for 10m after clew_startup_timestamp changes.

alarms.AlarmDescription: ConversationManager miss rate increasing at +5 percentage points per minute over 15 minutes. Indicates progressive degradation, not a one-time spike.
alarms.Notes: Requires CloudWatch Math Expression: RATE(miss_rate) > 0.05 over 15 1-minute periods. Suppress for 20m after clew_startup_timestamp changes.

alarms.AlarmDescription: Active conversation threads >200 sustained 10 minutes. Approaching Slack API rate limit territory. Review traffic patterns and consider scaling.
alarms.Notes: No post-deploy suppression needed. High thread count after deploy is expected (all threads are new). Concern is sustained high count indicating organic traffic spike.

alarms.AlarmDescription: Zero evictions with >50 active threads and >2h uptime. Eviction cleanup goroutine may be stalled. Thread memory will grow unbounded.
alarms.Notes: Compound condition: evictions_total == 0 AND active_threads > 50 AND (now - startup_timestamp) > 7200s. Requires CloudWatch Math Expression or composite alarm. Suppress for first 2h after deploy (no threads have expired yet).

alarms.AlarmDescription: KnowledgeIndex build P95 latency >75 seconds. Dangerously close to the 90s ECS health check timeout. Risk of rolling deploy loop.
alarms.Notes: This metric fires at container startup. P95 >75s means the build is routinely approaching the 90s timeout. Check: how many domains are reindexing, are LLM APIs slow, is content volume growing.

alarms.AlarmDescription: More than 10 stale fallbacks during KnowledgeIndex build. Domains are using outdated data because fresh content or LLM summarization failed.
alarms.Notes: Stale fallbacks indicate content load or LLM summarization failures. Check clew_build_knowledge_failures_total{type} for which phase is failing. Non-urgent but degraded quality.

alarms.AlarmDescription: One or more indexed domains are missing from the ContentStore. The KnowledgeIndex has metadata but no actual content for these domains.
alarms.Notes: Indicates a registry/content sync issue. The catalog lists domains that have no content files. Fix: regenerate content via ari know or check the content bake step in the Docker build.

alarms.AlarmDescription: Stage 4 (content load) reported a cache_miss source label. In Tier 1, all content is pre-loaded at startup so cache_miss should never occur. This indicates a ContentStore coherence issue.
alarms.Notes: Tier 1 only alert. In Tier 2, cache_miss is expected and this alert should be disabled. Filter on source=cache_miss dimension. Any nonzero count indicates a pre-load coherence bug.
